mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 1.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 1.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite unemployment spike, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflationary pressure that compresses real consumer purchasing power and future corporate revenues, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month while CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 0.5% signals weakening economic activity and reduced corporate capacity utilization, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines > 0.5% are positive in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust demand-side momentum and consumer confidence that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 5% month-over-month and PAYEMS rises > 150k month-over-month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered instances, or the joint interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3.5% year-over-year, the central bank is in restrictive tightening mode that suppresses equity valuations and forward earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) exceeding 200k persons signals strong labor market momentum and rising consumer income that supports aggregate demand and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in months with PAYEMS > 200k are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP (GDPC1) exceeding 0.5% signals economic contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations and increases recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors reduce equity exposure.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances following GDPC1 declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
